bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me only moderate utility, and with no messages or concession pattern yet, I will anchor on my strongest values across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s second offer is actually worse for my utility than their first, so I do not read meaningful concession from them; I will only concede on low-weight issueD, where they have consistently proposed valueC.

I am targeting very high utility because it is still extremely early (t=0.04) and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession; their offers to me remain low and centered on issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueC. Since they returned to their initial pattern rather than improving, I will hold firm on my three high-weight issues and avoid repeating my immediately previous bid by moving issueD back to my preferred value.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 rather than making major concessions. The opponent’s offers are valid but low for me and alternate between similar bids without real concession, while no messages provide additional credible signals; I therefore concede only on the low-weight issueD to valueC and hold firm on the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent’s offers have not improved in utility to me, staying around 0.40–0.44. Their bids suggest they may prefer issueC/valueC and issueD/valueC, but those concessions are not worth making further this early, especially on high-weight issues. I will return to my maximum-utility package rather than concede against a mostly firm opponent.

At t=0.10 it is still early, and the opponent’s offers have not improved my utility, alternating around low-value bids for me. I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making a small concession on the least important issue, issueD, which the opponent has repeatedly set to valueC.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my last offer. The opponent has slightly improved for me on issueA in their latest offer but remains far from my preferred package, so I will hold firm on the three high-weight issues and vary only the low-weight issueD.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility bid while making a noticeable concession from my maximum. The opponent has moved toward my preference on issueA but consistently offers valueC on issueC and issueD, with issueB at valueB/valueC; I will concede issueB and issueD while holding firm on high-value issueC.

I am targeting a strong utility bid around 0.88 by holding the high-weight gains the opponent just conceded on issueA and issueB, while making a moderate concession on issueC and accepting their recurring issueD preference. Since it is still early at t=0.16, I do not need to drop all the way to the opponent’s last offer, but their movement suggests issueA and issueB may now be negotiable while issueC remains their main sticking point.

I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a meaningful concession from my last offer because the opponent has recently conceded on issueA and issueB but is resisting my preferred issueC value. At t=0.18 there is still time, but their prior offer of issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA suggests we can hold those important issues while conceding issueC to a value they have already offered and issueD to their recurring value.

At t=0.20 I am still targeting a high-utility bid, since there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent has shown real concession on issueA and issueB and once offered issueA:valueA, issueB:valueA, issueC:valueC, issueD:valueC, but they seem resistant on issueC and consistently prefer issueD:valueC, so I concede issueD and partially concede issueC while holding firm on the two highest-value issues.

The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and issueB, with their latest valid offer worth about 0.811 to me, but it is still early at t=0.22 so I can target a higher-utility agreement. I am keeping my top values on the most important issues where the opponent has already moved, while conceding only the low-weight issueD to valueC and testing whether they can improve issueC.

I am targeting a high but no longer maximal utility bid of about 0.877, since the negotiation is still early at t=0.24 but the opponent has clearly moved toward my preferred issueA and issueB values. Their offers suggest they care about keeping issueD at valueC and resisting my top issueC value, so I concede issueD and soften issueC while holding firm on the two highest-value aligned issues.

I targeted a high-utility bid around 0.877, holding firm on the high-weight issues issueA and issueB while conceding issueD to the opponent’s consistent valueC. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and issueB but remains resistant on issueC, so at t=0.26 I make a moderate concession from my ideal issueC=valueB to issueC=valueA rather than dropping all the way to valueC yet.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still early-to-mid negotiation at t=0.28, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent has already moved toward my preferred values on issueA and sometimes issueB, while consistently holding issueD at valueC, which is a low-cost concession for me. I will hold firm on the high-weight issueC at my best value while keeping issueD at their apparent preference.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 because we are still early-mid negotiation at t=0.30, so there is no need to drop immediately to the opponent’s last offer. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and issueB, but continues to resist my top issueC value, so I am offering a compromise on issueC while keeping the high-weight issues A and B at my best values; there are no messages to adjust for.

At t=0.32 I am targeting a strong but more realistic utility around 0.81 by holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B while conceding on issueC and keeping their apparent preferred issueD valueC. The opponent has repeatedly offered issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA recently, but continues to push issueC toward valueC/valueD, so offering their recent best valid bid to me may improve acceptance without dropping too far this early-mid stage.
